remember: Students will be able to define a linked list and its components
Students will be able to recall the types of linked lists (singly, doubly, circular)
Students will be able to identify the advantages and disadvantages of linked lists
understand: Students will be able to explain the structure and operations of a linked list
Students will be able to understand how nodes are created, inserted, deleted, and traversed in a linked list
Students will be able to comprehend the concept of pointer variables in a linked list
apply: Students will be able to implement a singly linked list in a programming language
Students will be able to insert and delete nodes in a linked list
Students will be able to traverse a linked list using different traversal methods
analyze: Students will be able to compare the efficiency of linked lists with other data structures (arrays, stacks, queues)
Students will be able to analyze the time and space complexity of linked list operations
Students will be able to evaluate the use of linked lists in real-world applications

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT, students should be able to explain the concept of linked lists and their applications
Students should be able to understand the structure and operations of a linked list
Students should be able to implement a linked list in a programming language
board_exam_objectives: Students should be able to explain the concept of linked lists and their types
Students should be able to describe the advantages and disadvantages of linked lists
Students should be able to implement a linked list and demonstrate its operations
